other_observers: On 12/23 Brandon Percival and Peter Gaede On 12/29 Mark Peterson; Based on the distinctive bill coloration this is most likely the same individual first reported from the South Shore Marina by Brandon Percival in early December
specific_location: Lake Pueblo State Park, North Shore Marina
description: This is a small gull, slightly smaller than a Ring-billed Gull with which it associated.  Distincive features were mantle coloration, about two shades darker than nearby RBGU, bill thickness, thinner than RBGU, all yellow and short, except an distinctive black mark which makes this bird unique in appearance. The tertial crescents are broad white and contrast nicely with the mantle. The feet were yellowish. The neck streaking was distinctive with thick, closely spaced smudges on the hindneck, also noticeable different from RBGU.Behaviors: Sat on a tire the entire time I observed on both days.Call: none heardPlumage: adult basic
